title: Review Implement Verdict-First Review/Test Result Handling

handoff_summary.completed: No findings discovered. I verified that `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py` now injects explicit `verdict` / `findings_count` / `requires_followup` instructions for `review` and `tester`, `templates/agents/review.md` and `templates/agents/tester.md` require those fields, and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` covers both the prompt contract and template expectations. `python3 -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator` passed (95 tests, OK).
handoff_summary.risks: Residual downstream risk is limited to the intentional compatibility path in `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`; follow-up changes should not remove `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` or its warning-path coverage unless the migration is being intentionally completed end-to-end in the same change.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Shared-risk areas remain `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` review/test completion behavior. Downstream agents should preserve compat fallback semantics and warning coverage unless they are intentionally removing the fallback as part of a coordinated migration.

conflict_risks: Shared-risk areas remain `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` review/test completion behavior. Downstream agents should preserve compat fallback semantics and warning coverage unless they are intentionally removing the fallback as part of a coordinated migration.

execution_history.summary: Review found one correctness gap: the runtime supports structured review/test verdicts, but the worker prompts/guardrails still do not require those fields, so the spec is not fully implemented and legacy compat mode remains the default path for current review/test agents.
Created corrective bead B0056-review-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B0056-review-corrective completed
Review completed with no unresolved findings. Structured verdict requirements are now present in the review/tester guardrails and injected into worker prompts, with regression coverage in place.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review changed files and call out bugs, regressions, missing tests, and documentation gaps.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, and include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review Implement Verdict-First Review/Test Result Handling
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: Shared-risk areas remain `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` review/test completion behavior. Follow-up work should avoid removing `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` or its compat-warning coverage unless it is intentionally completing the full prompt migration in the same change.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed the B0056 implementation, docs, and regression coverage across `src/codex_orchestrator/models.py`, `src/codex_orchestrator/runner.py`, `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`, `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`, `templates/agents/review.md`, `templates/agents/tester.md`, `tests/test_orchestrator.py`, and the linked specs. Ran `python3 -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator` successfully (92 tests, OK).
handoff_summary.remaining: Finding 1 (medium): `specs/structured-review-verdict-v1.md` requires prompt/guardrail updates so review/test agents emit structured verdict output, but the implementation only adds optional schema fields in `src/codex_orchestrator/runner.py:22-24` and still gives generic prompt instructions in `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py:78-80`; the agent templates at `templates/agents/review.md:15-17` and `templates/agents/tester.md:15-17` never require `verdict`, `findings_count`, or `requires_followup`. As shipped, migrated agents are not actually instructed to send the new fields, so the system continues to rely on `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` for normal operation instead of only as a temporary fallback.
handoff_summary.risks: Without prompt/template enforcement, review/test runs can keep omitting structured verdict fields indefinitely, which preserves the old free-text fallback path, weakens the token-efficiency goal, and leaves the spec deliverable for prompt/guardrail updates incomplete.
handoff_summary.next_action: Update the review/test worker prompt path so those agents are explicitly required to emit `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`, then add/adjust tests to prove the prompt contract is present alongside the runtime compat fallback.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Prompt/guardrail updates required by the structured verdict spec were not implemented, so review/test agents are still not instructed to emit the new structured verdict fields.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Shared-risk areas remain `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` review/test completion behavior. Follow-up work should avoid removing `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` or its compat-warning coverage unless it is intentionally completing the full prompt migration in the same change.

last_agent_result.summary: Review completed with no unresolved findings. Structured verdict requirements are now present in the review/tester guardrails and injected into worker prompts, with regression coverage in place.
